# Intake notes — Wanjiku & Associates

- Article 22 / 23 petition track. Filing deadline target: 14 days from arrest
  (24 June 2024) → 8 July 2024 at the outside.
- Six petitioners: Brian Kamau, Faith Wanjiku, Musa Hassan, Cynthia Akinyi,
  Kevin Mutua, Aisha Mohammed. Names are demo-fictional.
- Respondents: Inspector General; OCS Central, Kamukunji, Pangani,
  Industrial Area, Buruburu, Kileleshwa; Honourable Attorney-General.
- Conduct complained of:
  - Arrest without charge (24/06/2024).
  - Detention beyond 24 hours without production (Article 49(1)(f)).
  - Denial of access to counsel (Article 49(1)(c) and Article 50).
  - Use of force at protest (Article 33, 37; CRD v Republic line).
  - Cross-station shuffling preventing family contact (right to dignity,
    Article 28).
  - Medical neglect (right to health, Article 43, where applicable).
- Reliefs to seek:
  - Conservatory order for production / release (Mutunga r. 23).
  - Declaration of constitutional violations.
  - Compensation under Article 23(3)(e).
  - Costs.
- Verification status:
  - OB extracts: collected from 6 stations, partially redacted.
  - WhatsApp transcripts: timestamped, chain-of-custody noted.
  - Swahili voice note: transcribed by Whisper, manual review pending.
  - Medical report: clinician available for deposition.
- Open items:
  - Phone photos with EXIF metadata (still queued for OCR / metadata
    extraction).
  - Confirm petitioner identifiers and ID numbers before filing.
  - Verify every Kenya Law citation by URL fetch — Verda kenyalaw-mcp will
    flag any unverified cite.
